evals.prompt: Set up authentication and role-based access control for my Power Pages React site. I need login/logout with Microsoft Entra ID and want to restrict certain pages to Administrators and Authenticated Users roles.
evals.expected_output: Full auth setup for a React site including auth service with login/logout, type declarations, role-based authorization utilities, AuthButton component integrated into navigation, and role-based UI patterns for Administrators and Authenticated Users. ProfileRedirectEnabled site setting configured and deployment prompted.
expectations: Detects the site as a React framework project
Discovers existing web roles including Administrators and Authenticated Users
Asks which identity provider to use and user selects Microsoft Entra ID
Creates a powerPages.d.ts type declarations file for the shell API
Creates an auth service module with AUTH_PROVIDER configured for entra-id type
Auth service login function posts to /Account/Login/ExternalLogin with Entra ID provider URL
Creates a React-specific hook (e.g., useAuth) for consuming auth state in components
Creates mock data or local dev helpers so auth works in local development
Creates authorization utility functions for checking user roles
Creates React-specific authorization components (e.g., RoleGuard or RequireRole)
Creates an AuthButton component that shows login/logout based on auth state
Integrates the AuthButton component into the site navigation
Applies role-based UI patterns to protect content for Administrators and Authenticated Users
Runs a build verification to confirm no compilation errors
Sets the ProfileRedirectEnabled site setting to false
Prompts the user to deploy the updated site

evals.prompt: Add login and logout functionality to my Vue Power Pages site using Microsoft Entra ID. I don't need any role-based access control, just basic authentication so users can sign in and out.
evals.expected_output: Auth setup for a Vue site with login/logout only. Auth service, Vue composable, AuthButton component in navigation, type declarations, and mock data for local dev. No role-based authorization utilities or protected content patterns. ProfileRedirectEnabled configured and deployment prompted.
expectations: Detects the site as a Vue framework project
Checks for existing web roles but does not require them for login-only setup
Creates a powerPages.d.ts type declarations file for the shell API
Creates an auth service module with login and logout functions using Microsoft Entra ID
Creates a Vue-specific composable (e.g., useAuth) for consuming auth state
Creates mock data or local dev helpers so auth works in local development
Does not create role-checking authorization utility functions
Does not create role-based guard components or directives
Creates an AuthButton component that shows login/logout based on auth state
Integrates the AuthButton component into the site navigation
Runs a build verification to confirm no compilation errors
Sets the ProfileRedirectEnabled site setting to false
Prompts the user to deploy the updated site

Runs a build verification to confirm no compilation errors

evals.prompt: Set up authentication and authorization for my Power Pages React site. I want role-based access for different user groups.
evals.expected_output: The skill detects that no web roles exist on the site and informs the user that web roles must be created in the Power Pages admin center before role-based authorization can be configured. The skill should not proceed with authorization setup until roles are available.
expectations: Detects the site as a React framework project
Checks for existing web roles and finds none configured
Informs the user that no web roles exist on the site
Prompts or instructs the user to create web roles in Power Pages admin center before proceeding
Does not create authorization utilities or role-based components without valid web roles
May still offer to set up basic login/logout authentication without role-based access

Creates site settings for Authentication/OpenIdConnect/{provider}/RedirectUri
Creates a powerPages.d.ts type declarations file
Creates AuthButton component with 'Sign in with Okta' display text
Sets the ProfileRedirectEnabled site setting to false
Prompts the user to deploy the updated site

evals.prompt: I want to set up local username and password authentication for my Power Pages React site. No external identity provider needed, just simple email/password login.
evals.expected_output: Auth setup for a React site using local authentication. Auth service configured with local provider type that posts credentials to /SignIn (the server-rendered login form endpoint) — NOT to /Account/Login/Login and NOT to /Account/Login/ExternalLogin. The form sends the password as the `PasswordValue` field (not `Password`). Includes a LocalLoginForm component with username and password fields. Site settings enable local login.
expectations: Detects the site as a React framework project
Asks which identity provider to use and user selects Local Authentication
Creates an auth service with AUTH_PROVIDER configured for local type
Auth service login function posts to /SignIn (the server-rendered login form endpoint) — NOT to /Account/Login/Login and NOT to /Account/Login/ExternalLogin

Auth service login function sends the password as the form field named PasswordValue (NOT Password) — Power Pages' /SignIn endpoint uses PasswordValue; the Password field name is only used by the separate /Account/Login/Register endpoint
Creates a LocalLoginForm component with username/email and password input fields
LocalLoginForm handles form submission and error display
Creates site settings for Authentication/Registration/LocalLoginEnabled set to true
Creates site settings for Authentication/Registration/LocalLoginByEmail set to true
Creates a powerPages.d.ts type declarations file
Creates mock data or local dev helpers so auth works in local development
Sets the ProfileRedirectEnabled site setting to false
Prompts the user to deploy the updated site

evals.prompt: Add Google and Facebook social login to my Power Pages Angular site. I want users to be able to sign in with either provider.
evals.expected_output: Auth setup for an Angular site with Google and Facebook as individual providers. Auth service supports multiple social providers using AUTH_PROVIDERS array with loginWithProvider(). Site settings created for both Google and Facebook with collected client IDs and secrets via environment variables. Login UI shows both provider buttons.

Recognizes user wants both Google and Facebook providers
Asks user for the Google Client ID from Google Cloud Console
Asks user for the Facebook App ID from Facebook Developer Console
Creates an auth service with AUTH_PROVIDERS array for multi-provider support
Auth service includes loginWithProvider() function for provider-specific login
Auth service supports login with provider identifiers for both Google and Facebook
Creates site settings for Authentication/OpenAuth/Google/ClientId with collected value
Creates site settings for Authentication/OpenAuth/Facebook/AppId with collected value (not ClientId)
Creates ClientSecret and AppSecret as environment variables (not in YAML files)
Warns user not to commit ClientSecret or AppSecret values to source control
Creates auth UI that presents both Google and Facebook as login buttons
Creates a powerPages.d.ts type declarations file
Creates Angular-specific injectable service for auth state
Sets the ProfileRedirectEnabled site setting to false
Prompts the user to deploy the updated site

evals.prompt: Set up local login with two-factor authentication for my Power Pages React site. Users should log in with email and password, then verify with a 2FA code.
evals.expected_output: Auth setup for a React site with local login only. Skill explains that Power Pages built-in 2FA is not supported in SPA-based code sites because the SendCode/VerifyCode pages are server-rendered and cannot be intercepted from the SPA. Recommends enabling MFA at the identity provider layer instead (or accepting that local-only 2FA is not currently scaffolded). Proceeds with the rest of local auth setup without creating any TwoFactorEnabled / RememberMeEnabled / RememberBrowserEnabled site settings.
expectations: Detects the site as a React framework project
Tells the user that Power Pages built-in 2FA is not supported in SPA-based code sites because SendCode/VerifyCode are server-rendered and cannot be intercepted
Recommends enabling MFA at the identity provider layer (Entra External ID conditional access, B2C user flow MFA, etc.) instead, OR accepting local-only sign-in without 2FA for now
Does NOT offer 2FA as an option in the Phase 2.1 optional features question
Does NOT create Authentication/Registration/TwoFactorEnabled site setting
Does NOT create Authentication/Registration/RememberMeEnabled site setting
Does NOT create Authentication/Registration/RememberBrowserEnabled site setting
Creates an auth service with AUTH_PROVIDER configured for local type with loginByEmail set to true
Auth service login function posts Email field (not Username) when loginByEmail is true
Creates LocalLoginForm component with email field and password field
Creates site settings for Authentication/Registration/LocalLoginEnabled set to true
Creates site settings for Authentication/Registration/LocalLoginByEmail set to true
Creates a powerPages.d.ts type declarations file
Sets the ProfileRedirectEnabled site setting to false
Prompts the user to deploy the updated site

evals.prompt: Configure invitation-based registration for my Power Pages Vue site with Microsoft Entra ID. Only users with invitation codes should be able to register.
evals.expected_output: Auth setup for a Vue site with Entra ID and invitation-based registration. Auth service supports passing invitation codes through the login flow. Site settings configure invitation-only registration by setting Authentication/Registration/InvitationEnabled = true AND Authentication/Registration/OpenRegistrationEnabled = false (this combination is what the server enforces; there is no RequireInvitationCode setting). Explains how invitation codes are threaded through the ExternalLogin flow.
expectations: Detects the site as a Vue framework project
Creates an auth service with AUTH_PROVIDER configured for entra-id type
Auth service login function accepts and passes through invitationCode parameter
Creates site settings for Authentication/Registration/InvitationEnabled set to true

Creates site settings for Authentication/Registration/OpenRegistrationEnabled set to false (this combination — InvitationEnabled=true + OpenRegistrationEnabled=false — is what enforces invitation-only registration on the server)
Does NOT create Authentication/Registration/RequireInvitationCode — this name is not a real Power Pages server setting; the server never reads it
Explains the invitation code URL format for sharing with users
Explains that invitationCode is passed as a query parameter to ExternalLogin
Creates a Vue-specific composable for consuming auth state
Creates a powerPages.d.ts type declarations file
Sets the ProfileRedirectEnabled site setting to false
Prompts the user to deploy the updated site

evals.prompt: Set up OpenID Connect authentication for my Power Pages React site with Okta. Authority URL is https://dev-12345.okta.com/oauth2/default. I also need custom claims mapping so that the user's department and job title are synced to the contact record on registration.
evals.expected_output: Auth setup for a React site using OIDC with Okta. Asks for ClientId during the flow. When user opts for advanced settings, creates RegistrationClaimsMapping site setting with a comma-separated string of `contactfield=claimtype` pairs (NOT JSON) mapping OIDC claims to Dataverse contact fields. Session timeout and other defaults left as-is.
expectations: Detects the site as a React framework project

Offers advanced settings configuration and user opts in
Presents the OIDC optional settings table including RegistrationClaimsMapping and LoginClaimsMapping

Creates site settings for Authentication/OpenIdConnect/{provider}/RegistrationClaimsMapping with a comma-separated string of `contactfield=claimtype` pairs (NOT JSON) — the Power Pages server parses CSV, not JSON, for this setting

Claims mapping value maps department and job title OIDC claims to Dataverse contact fields using the comma-separated CSV format, e.g. `firstname=given_name,lastname=family_name,emailaddress1=email,department=department,jobtitle=job_title`
Creates all required OIDC site settings (Authority, ClientId, AuthenticationType, RedirectUri)
Handles ClientSecret via environment variable (not in YAML)
Creates a powerPages.d.ts type declarations file
Sets the ProfileRedirectEnabled site setting to false
Prompts the user to deploy the updated site

evals.prompt: I already set up Entra External ID for my Power Pages React site but users are seeing an error page after login. The URL shows ?message=access_denied. How do I handle this?
evals.expected_output: The skill recognizes this is about auth error handling. The getAuthError() function in authService parses ?message= query params and maps them to user-friendly messages. The access_denied code maps to 'Access was denied by the identity provider.' The login page or AuthButton should call getAuthError() on mount and display the error inline.
expectations: Recognizes this is about error handling for an existing auth setup
Explains that getAuthError() parses ?message= and ?error= query params
Shows the AUTH_ERROR_MESSAGES map or explains what access_denied maps to
Explains that the error should be displayed inline in the SPA (not on a server page)
Shows how to use getAuthError() in a React component (useEffect on mount)
Mentions getSessionExpiredMessage() for ?sessionExpired=true handling
Does NOT suggest redirecting to server-rendered error pages

evals.prompt: Set up local authentication for my Power Pages React site in invitation-only mode. Users should only be able to register if they have a valid invitation code emailed by an admin. Direct self-registration should be blocked.
evals.expected_output: Local auth setup for a React site with invitation-only registration mode. Creates registration mode site settings (Enabled=true, OpenRegistrationEnabled=false, InvitationEnabled=true), creates the SPA redeem-invitation page, extends the Code-Site-Shell-Header redirect map with /account/login/redeeminvitation, adds redeemInvitation() and fetchInvitationDetails() to auth service, updates loginLocal() to accept invitationCode, updates Login page to read invitationCode from URL and show banner, Registration page pre-fills email via fetchInvitationDetails. Does NOT create the RequireInvitationCode site setting (phantom setting that the server does not read).
expectations: Detects the site as a React framework project
Asks the registration mode question and user selects Invitation-only
Creates an auth service with AUTH_PROVIDER configured for local type
Adds redeemInvitation(invitationCode, redeemByLogin, returnUrl) function to authService
redeemInvitation uses fetch() with redirect:'manual' to intercept the server 302
redeemInvitation returns { nextStep: 'register' } when response.type === 'opaqueredirect'
redeemInvitation returns { nextStep: 'login' } when the response HTML contains login form markers
redeemInvitation throws parsed errors when the response HTML contains validation summary errors
Adds fetchInvitationDetails(invitationCode) function to authService that parses email from #EmailTextBox
Updates loginLocal() to accept an invitationCode 5th parameter and append it as ?InvitationCode= on /SignIn URL
Creates a /redeem-invitation SPA page that pre-fills code from the URL and shows a 'Sign in with an existing account instead of registering' checkbox
RedeemInvitation page navigates to /registration?invitationCode= or /login?invitationCode= based on redeemInvitation result
Creates a /registration SPA page (still created in invitation-only mode — reached via redeem-invitation flow)
Registration page calls fetchInvitationDetails(invitationCode) on mount when invitationCode is in URL and pre-fills the email input
Updates Login page to read invitationCode from URL, show an info banner, and pass invitationCode through to loginLocal()
Extends Code-Site-Shell-Header template redirect map to include '/account/login/redeeminvitation': '/redeem-invitation'
Creates site setting Authentication/Registration/Enabled = true
Creates site setting Authentication/Registration/OpenRegistrationEnabled = false
Creates site setting Authentication/Registration/InvitationEnabled = true
Does NOT create the Authentication/Registration/RequireInvitationCode site setting (phantom — server never reads it)
Creates site settings for LocalLoginEnabled, LocalLoginByEmail, ResetPasswordEnabled
Creates CAPTCHA-disabled site settings (CaptchaEnabled=false, IsCaptchaEnabledForRegistration=false)
Sets the ProfileRedirectEnabled site setting to false
Post-deploy notes show the correct invitation link format: {site-url}/Account/Login/RedeemInvitation?invitation={code}
Prompts the user to deploy the updated site

evals.prompt: Set up local authentication for my Power Pages React site with both open registration AND invitation-based registration enabled. Users should be able to self-register OR redeem an invitation link.
evals.expected_output: Local auth setup for a React site with both open and invitation modes enabled. Creates registration mode site settings (Enabled=true, OpenRegistrationEnabled=true, InvitationEnabled=true). Creates both the /registration and /redeem-invitation SPA pages. Registration page accepts direct navigation AND invitation links (pre-fills email when invitationCode is present).
expectations: Detects the site as a React framework project
Asks the registration mode question and user selects Both
Creates site setting Authentication/Registration/Enabled = true
Creates site setting Authentication/Registration/OpenRegistrationEnabled = true
Creates site setting Authentication/Registration/InvitationEnabled = true
Does NOT create the RequireInvitationCode site setting
Creates a /registration SPA page accessible both directly and via invitation flow
Registration page pre-fills email via fetchInvitationDetails() when invitationCode is in URL, blank otherwise
Creates a /redeem-invitation SPA page
Extends Code-Site-Shell-Header redirect map with /account/login/redeeminvitation -> /redeem-invitation
Adds redeemInvitation() and fetchInvitationDetails() to auth service
Sets the ProfileRedirectEnabled site setting to false
Prompts the user to deploy the updated site

evals.prompt: Set up local authentication for my Power Pages React site but DISABLE new user registration entirely. Only existing users should be able to log in — no one should be able to create new accounts via the site.
evals.expected_output: Local auth setup for a React site with registration disabled mode. Creates the Authentication/Registration/Enabled = false site setting. Does NOT create /registration page, /redeem-invitation page, or invitation/open-registration settings. Login, forgot password, reset password pages still created for existing users.
expectations: Detects the site as a React framework project
Asks the registration mode question and user selects Registration disabled
Creates site setting Authentication/Registration/Enabled = false
Does NOT create site settings for OpenRegistrationEnabled or InvitationEnabled (they are moot when Enabled=false)
Does NOT create the RequireInvitationCode site setting
Does NOT create a /registration SPA page
Does NOT create a /redeem-invitation SPA page
Does NOT include /account/login/redeeminvitation in the Code-Site-Shell-Header redirects
Still creates the /login page for existing users
Still creates /forgot-password and /reset-password pages (existing users may still need password resets)
Sets the ProfileRedirectEnabled site setting to false
Prompts the user to deploy the updated site

evals.prompt: Set up authentication for my Power Pages React site with Entra External ID, and add a profile page so signed-in users can edit their basic contact info (name, phone, address).
evals.expected_output: Full auth setup for a React site with Entra External ID PLUS a simple /user-profile SPA page. Skill scaffolds UserProfile.tsx (with a read-only Account Details section showing just full name + email, plus an edit form limited to firstname, lastname, mobilephone, and 5 address fields), extends authService.ts with getMyProfile and updateMyProfile, evolves AuthButton to a dropdown with My Profile + Sign Out items, adds /user-profile route, writes Webapi/contact/enabled and Webapi/contact/fields site settings (9 entries, emailaddress1 and middlename excluded), and creates a Self-scope (756150004) table permission on contact for the Authenticated Users role.
expectations: Asks the registration mode question and user selects an option that creates contacts (Open registration / Both / Invitation-only)
Asks the new profile-page question in Phase 2.1 and user selects Yes
Captures INCLUDE_PROFILE_PAGE = true
Phase 5.1.9 creates src/pages/UserProfile.tsx at exactly that path and filename (NOT Profile.tsx, NOT /profile route)
Route is /user-profile (NOT /profile — /profile is reserved by the server)
Service functions are named getMyProfile and updateMyProfile (NOT patchOwnContact, fetchProfile, saveProfile, etc.)

UserProfile page sets document.title in a useEffect using the same `<Page> — <Site Name>` convention as other auth pages (e.g., 'My Profile — Contoso Portal'), with the actual site name substituted in
UserProfile page reads user.contactId from useAuth() and calls getMyProfile(contactId) on mount

UserProfile page has a read-only Account Details section at the top showing ONLY the user's full name (firstname + lastname combined) and email (sourced from useAuth().user.email — no Web API roundtrip needed)
Account Details section does NOT display contactId, web roles, claims, or any other metadata — only full name + email

UserProfile page renders editable form fields limited to exactly 8 fields: firstname, lastname, mobilephone, address1_line1, address1_city, address1_stateorprovince, address1_postalcode, address1_country
UserProfile page does NOT include an editable email field (emailaddress1 is read-only in Account Details, never in the form)
UserProfile page does NOT include a middlename field
UserProfile page does NOT include a Change Password / Reset Password link or any password-related UI
UserProfile page does NOT include a Sign Out button (sign-out stays in the header AuthButton dropdown)
UserProfile page uses validate-on-blur pattern matching Login.tsx / Registration.tsx (with phone format check)
UserProfile page handles user.contactId being empty by showing a friendly error pointing to RegistrationClaimsMapping

Extends src/services/authService.ts with ProfileContact interface (8 editable fields + contactid — NO emailaddress1, NO middlename), ProfileUpdate type, getMyProfile function, and updateMyProfile function
updateMyProfile PATCHes /_api/contacts({contactId}) with __RequestVerificationToken header and If-Match: * header
updateMyProfile omits undefined fields from the body so partial updates don't clear other columns
Updates src/components/AuthButton.tsx to a dropdown shape with state-based open/close, outside-click handler, Escape key handler, and ARIA attributes
Dropdown shows 'My Profile' link to /user-profile and 'Sign Out' button when INCLUDE_PROFILE_PAGE is true
Adds <Route path='/user-profile' element={<UserProfile />} /> to src/App.tsx

Adds '/signin': '/login' to the Code-Site-Shell-Header template's redirect map so that when the server bounces unauthenticated users from server-protected paths (e.g., /profile) to /SignIn?ReturnUrl=..., they land on the SPA /login page instead of the legacy server sign-in UI

Extends src/services/authService.ts with an applyContactUpdateLocally(payload) helper that mirrors saved firstname/lastname back into window.Microsoft.Dynamic365.Portal.User (mutating firstName/lastName camelCase properties on the snapshot) so the header reflects the new values without a page reload

Updates src/hooks/useAuth.ts (or framework equivalent) so refresh()/loadUser() spreads the user object into a fresh reference — e.g., setUser(current ? { ...current } : undefined) — so React detects the state change after Portal.User is mutated in place
After successful save, UserProfile calls applyContactUpdateLocally(payload) BEFORE refresh() so the header avatar/display name in AuthButton repaint immediately without a full page reload
After successful save, UserProfile calls refresh() from useAuth() and shows inline 'Profile updated.' message — does not redirect or reload the page
Phase 8.1 writes Webapi/contact/enabled = true site setting

Phase 8.1 writes Webapi/contact/fields site setting with exactly 9 lowercase logical names (contactid,firstname,lastname,mobilephone,address1_line1,address1_city,address1_stateorprovince,address1_postalcode,address1_country) — emailaddress1 and middlename are NOT in this list
Phase 8.1.x discovers the Authenticated Users web role UUID from .powerpages-site/web-roles/
Phase 8.1.x creates table permission YAML at .powerpages-site/table-permissions/My-Profile-Edit-Own-Contact.tablepermission.yml
Table permission YAML has scope: 756150004 (Self), read: true, write: true, create: false, delete: false
Table permission YAML associates the Authenticated Users role UUID via adx_entitypermission_webrole array
Does NOT use Contact scope (756150001) for the contact entity (script validates and rejects this)
Sets the ProfileRedirectEnabled site setting to false
Generates an HTML setup report at <PROJECT_ROOT>/docs/auth-setup-report.html via render-auth-report.js, opens it in the browser, and tells the user the file path
Prompts the user to deploy the updated site

evals.prompt: Set up authentication for my Power Pages React site with local login and Terms and Conditions enabled.
evals.expected_output: Auth setup for a React site with local login + Terms and Conditions. Before asking the user about the Privacy Extensions solution prerequisite, the skill automatically runs check-solution-installed.js against the current Dataverse environment to detect whether msdynce_PortalPrivacyExtensions is installed. Based on the JSON result, the skill either confirms the prerequisite (and skips the question), or tells the user clearly that Terms & Conditions will NOT be enforced server-side and asks whether to continue scaffolding anyway or skip Terms entirely. If the auto-detection fails (no PAC env, expired token, missing permissions), the skill falls back to the manual three-option confirmation question.
expectations: Detects the site as a React framework project
Asks the optional features question in Phase 2.1 and the user selects Terms and Conditions
Runs `node "${PLUGIN_ROOT}/scripts/check-solution-installed.js" --solutionName "msdynce_PortalPrivacyExtensions"` BEFORE asking the GDPR prerequisite question
Parses the script's stdout JSON result and branches based on the `installed` boolean

When the script returns installed: true, skips the manual prerequisite confirmation entirely and tells the user the solution is detected (including the version from the JSON) before proceeding to collect Terms content

When the script returns installed: false, tells the user clearly that Terms and Conditions will NOT be enforced by the server until msdynce_PortalPrivacyExtensions is installed (Authentication/Registration/TermsAgreementEnabled is silently ignored without it), then asks via AskUserQuestion whether to continue scaffolding anyway or skip Terms entirely (two options, not three)

When the script exits non-zero (infrastructure failure: no env URL, expired Azure CLI token, missing Read permission on the solutions table, network error), tells the user we couldn't auto-detect the solution and falls back to the manual three-option question (Yes / Continue anyway / Cancel)
Sets the ProfileRedirectEnabled site setting to false

Generates an HTML setup report at <PROJECT_ROOT>/docs/auth-setup-report.html via render-auth-report.js; OPTIONAL_FEATURES_DATA.termsEnforced reflects the detected solution state (true if installed, false otherwise)
Prompts the user to deploy the updated site
